sections.paragraph:
-

(1) f. of the preceding, the female ibex, or chamois. Pro 5:19, יַעֲלַת חֵן “the graceful chamois;” an epithet applied to a beautiful woman. The Arabs say proverbially ازهى من الوعل more beautiful than an ibex; Bochart, i. 899.

(2) [Jaalah, Jahala], pr.n. m., Ezr 2:56 Neh 7:58. [יַעֲלָא].
